title: senecio woodsonianus
intro: Senecio woodsonianus, also known as Wood's Groundsel, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Senecio woodsonianus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and even exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for treating various ailments. Senecio woodsonianus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
